zh.Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾布利沃斯
zh.Bressingham, Norfolk, England: 英國諾福爾可布雷辛加姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight於1299年6月24日出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾布利沃斯時，他的父親Thomas de Verdun24歲，母親Margaret Knoville19歲。他和Margaret de Braose至少有1個兒子。他去世於1376年10月23日在英國諾福爾可布雷辛加姆享年77歲。

zh-hans.Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾布利沃斯
zh-hans.Bressingham, Norfolk, England: 英国諾福爾可布雷辛加姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight于1299年6月24日出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾布利沃斯时，他的父亲Thomas de Verdun24岁，母亲Margaret Knoville19岁。他和Margaret de Braose至少有1个儿子。他去世于1376年10月23日在英国諾福爾可布雷辛加姆享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

variants.name: Varden
meanings: English: variant of   Verdun  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: First
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of Fürst (see Furst ) and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): artificial name from Yiddish first ‘prince’.
Slovenian and Croatian (Firšt): nickname from firšt ‘prince’, an archaic or dialect loanword from German Fürst (see Furst and above).

variants.name: Furst
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Eugen, Kurt, Gunter, Hans, Herta, Maximilian, Ulrich.

German and Swiss German (Fürst): nickname from Old High German furisto ‘the first, most noble one’, a cognate of Old English fyrest ‘first, foremost’. The surname Fürst is also found elsewhere in central Europe, e.g. in Czechia and Slovenia. Compare Feerst , Fierce , Fierst , First , and Fuerst .
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Fürst): artificial name from German Fürst ‘prince’. Compare Fuerst .

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: Verdun,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

variants.name: Varden

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight was born on 24 June 1299, in Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Thomas de Verdun, was 24 and his mother, Margaret Knoville, was 19. He had at least 1 son with Margaret de Braose. He died on 23 October 1376, in Bressingham, Norfolk, England, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight est né le 24 juin 1299, à Brixworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Verdun, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret Knoville, avait 19 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Margaret de Braose. Il est décédé le 23 octobre 1376, à Bressingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight am 24. Juni 1299 in Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Verdun 24 und seine Mutter Margaret Knoville 19. Er hatte mit Margaret de Braose mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 23. Oktober 1376 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Bressingham, Norfolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight è nato il il 24 giugno 1299 nel Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas de Verdun, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Knoville, aveva 19 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Margaret de Braose. Morì il 23 ottobre 1376, nel Bressingham, Norfolk, England, all'età di 77 anni.

ja.Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイア
ja.Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England: イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリクスウオルス
ja.Bressingham, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクブレシンガム
ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knightが1299年6月24日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリクスウオルスで生まれた時，父Thomas de Verdunは24歳，母Margaret Knovilleは19歳であった。彼はMargaret de Braoseとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1376年10月23日にイギリスノーフォルクブレシンガムで77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight(이)가 1299년 6월 24일 영국 노르섬프토실르 브리스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Verdun(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Knoville(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Margaret de Braose(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1376년 10월 23일 영국 노르폴크 브레징함에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.24 June 1299–23 October 1376: 1299 оны Зургаадугаар сарын 24–1376 оны Аравдугаар сарын 23

pt.24 June 1299–23 October 1376: 24 de junho de 1299–23 de outubro de 1376
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight nasceu em 24 de junho de 1299, em Brixworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Verdun, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Knoville, tinha 19 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Margaret de Braose. Ele faleceu em 23 de outubro de 1376, em Bressingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 77 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight родился 24 июня 1299 г. в местности бриксворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas de Verdun было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Knoville, 19. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Margaret de Braose. Он умер 23 октября 1376 г. в местности брессингхам, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 77 лет.

es.24 June 1299–23 October 1376: 24 de junio de 1299–23 de octubre de 1376
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John de Verdun of Bressingham - First Baron de Verdun, Knight nació el 24 de junio de 1299, en Brixworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Verdun, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Margaret Knoville, tenía 19 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Margaret de Braose. Murió el 23 de octubre de 1376, en Bressingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 77 años.
